About.title: About Us
About.intro: {name} is a US-based fiber infrastructure contractor. We deliver from backbone and middle-mile to FTTH/FTTP and last-mile — for carriers, data centers, municipalities, and general contractors.
About.missionText: We focus on safe, compliant, and well-documented fiber construction. Our approach emphasizes schedule discipline, ROW and 811 compliance, restoration, and long-term asset durability. We work as an extension of your team with clear communication and closeout packages that meet carrier and municipal standards.
About.experienceText: {name} brings {years}+ years of operational fiber construction experience to every project. Our crews and project teams have built backbone, data center, FTTH, and municipal networks. We support nationwide projects where applicable.
About.nextStepsText: Request our prequalification package for your vendor files, or contact us to discuss your project.

Capabilities.title: Capabilities Statement
Capabilities.subtitle: Outside Plant (OSP) · Fiber Infrastructure · Field Operations
Capabilities.companyOverviewText: Cloud Telecommunications is a Texas-based company providing outside plant (OSP) and fiber infrastructure field operations support. Leadership experience dates back to 1977, beginning with direct Outside Plant (OSP) supervision and continuing through over four decades of complex field operations across telecommunications and energy environments. The company is founder-led and field-driven.
Capabilities.diff5: Proven Performance in Complex Field Environments
Capabilities.leadershipSummary: Terry Dickman began his telecommunications infrastructure career in 1977 as an Outside Plant Superintendent overseeing cable installation, network expansion, and field operations. Over the following decades, he led large-scale field operations across telecommunications and energy sectors, maintaining a consistent focus on safety, contractor management, and disciplined project execution. Cloud Telecommunications represents the continuation of this field-proven infrastructure leadership.
Capabilities.naics237130: Power and Communication Line and Related Structures Construction
Capabilities.naics238210: Electrical Contractors and Other Wiring Installation Contractors

Careers.title: Careers / Subcontractors
Careers.intro: Join our team as a W2 hire or partner as a subcontractor. We're building fiber infrastructure nationwide.
Careers.openRolesText: We hire foremen, HDD operators, fiber splicers, locators, and crew leads. Full-time and project-based positions.
Careers.subcontractorOnboardingText: We work with qualified subcontractors for fiber construction, splicing, and restoration. Requirements and documentation checklist:
Careers.checklist1: Insurance (general liability, workers comp) per project requirements
Careers.checklist3: Documentation: W-9, insurance certificates, safety docs
Careers.checklist4: Scope and rate agreement before mobilization

SafetyQuality.title: Safety & Quality
SafetyQuality.intro: OSHA, 811, ROW, TCP, and QC checkpoints. We build safety and documentation into every project.
SafetyQuality.section1Content: We maintain OSHA-compliant job sites with required PPE, signage, and training. Safety meetings and tailgates are standard. Incident reporting and corrective action follow established procedures.
SafetyQuality.section2Content: No excavation without 811 locates. We coordinate with call-before-you-dig and private locates as required. Respect for existing utilities is built into every phase of work.
SafetyQuality.section3Content: We obtain and follow municipal right-of-way permits and restoration requirements. Restoration is completed per municipal and client specifications.
SafetyQuality.section4Content: Lane closures and work zones follow approved TCPs. We work with certified flaggers and maintain traffic control per MUTCD and local requirements.
SafetyQuality.section5Content: OTDR and test packs, as-builts, and closeout documentation are part of our standard deliverables. We support client QC checkpoints and acceptance criteria throughout the project.

InternetWorld.title: Internet World
InternetWorld.subtitle: Global & U.S. Infrastructure Maps & Data
InternetWorld.intro: Explore how the internet is built: submarine cables, terrestrial fiber, data centers, and broadband coverage. All maps and statistics below are from third-party sources; we do not control their content or availability.
InternetWorld.dataDisclaimer: Data based on publicly available industry reports (ITU, FCC, Cloudflare Radar, etc.). Figures are informational approximations and may vary by source and date.
InternetWorld.backboneTitle: From Global Backbone to Local Deployment
InternetWorld.backboneText: The international backbone depends on submarine cable systems that land at coastal points and connect to terrestrial networks. In the U.S., the backbone distributes traffic through metro fiber and long-haul corridors. Last-mile FTTH and premises connectivity depend on local OSP construction—conduit, fiber placement, splicing, and testing.
InternetWorld.bullet1: HDD crossings for road and obstacle avoidance
InternetWorld.bullet4: Diverse routing for redundancy and resilience
InternetWorld.ctaText: Cloud Telecommunications delivers OSP construction for backbone, metro, and last-mile. Request an RFQ or explore our services.

DataCenterEnergyOptimization.metaDescription: Cloud Telecommunications improves energy efficiency for existing data centers using natural gas combined cycle power systems, waste heat recovery cooling, and geothermal cooling solutions utilizing abandoned oil wells.
DataCenterEnergyOptimization.heroTitle: Reducing Energy Costs for Modern Data Centers
DataCenterEnergyOptimization.heroSubtitle: CloudTelc helps data center operators reduce operating costs and improve sustainability through advanced energy optimization solutions including waste heat recovery cooling and geothermal systems.
DataCenterEnergyOptimization.heroImageAlt: Energy optimization for hyperscale data centers: natural gas combined cycle and waste heat cooling, geothermal cooling data center infrastructure, data center cooling efficiency.
DataCenterEnergyOptimization.modelTitle: How It Works — Two Optimization Systems
DataCenterEnergyOptimization.modelImageAlt: Data center energy optimization: natural gas combined cycle with waste heat recovery and absorption cooling; geothermal cooling using abandoned oil wells and subsurface heat exchange for hyperscale data centers.
DataCenterEnergyOptimization.modelCaption: Energy Optimization for Hyperscale Data Centers
DataCenterEnergyOptimization.modelVoiceoverVideoTitle: How the model improves energy efficiency (video with voiceover)
DataCenterEnergyOptimization.modelVoiceoverVideoCaption: Video with voiceover: How the model contributes to energy efficiency.
DataCenterEnergyOptimization.videoCaption: Data center energy optimization: waste heat recovery and geothermal cooling.
DataCenterEnergyOptimization.narrationTranscript: Data center energy optimization relies on two innovative processes: natural gas combined cycle absorption and subterranean heat exchange. The combined cycle absorption technology captures and converts high-grade waste heat into mechanical or cooling energy, eliminating the need for fossil fuels and increasing energy efficiency. For hyperscale data centers, a subterranean system utilizes the earth's natural thermal properties to store and retrieve thermal energy at varying temperatures for cooling and heating purposes. With subterranean heat exchange, ground-source heat pumps facilitate heat transfer between the data center and the stable-temperature ground, which is much more efficient than using ambient air. Together, combined cycle absorption and subterranean heat exchange technologies pave the way for sustainable and responsible data center operations.
DataCenterEnergyOptimization.challengeTitle: Energy is the Largest Cost in Data Centers
DataCenterEnergyOptimization.challengeText1: Energy consumption represents one of the largest operational costs for modern data centers. Cooling systems alone can account for a significant share of electricity usage.
DataCenterEnergyOptimization.challengeText2: CloudTelc develops infrastructure solutions that transform unused energy sources into efficient cooling systems for data center facilities.
DataCenterEnergyOptimization.solution1Title: Natural Gas Combined Cycle + Waste Heat Cooling
DataCenterEnergyOptimization.solution1Text1: CloudTelc deploys high-efficiency natural gas combined cycle systems to supply electricity to data centers.
DataCenterEnergyOptimization.solution1Text2: Unlike conventional power generation, the system captures waste heat generated during electricity production.
DataCenterEnergyOptimization.solution1Text3: This waste heat is then used to power absorption cooling systems that significantly reduce the energy required for traditional data center cooling.
DataCenterEnergyOptimization.solution2Title: Geothermal Cooling Using Abandoned Oil Wells
DataCenterEnergyOptimization.solution2Text1: Many regions contain inactive oil wells that can be repurposed as geothermal heat exchange systems.
DataCenterEnergyOptimization.solution2Text2: CloudTelc utilizes these existing underground structures to circulate cooling fluids through deep subsurface formations.
DataCenterEnergyOptimization.solution2Text3: The geothermal system provides stable temperature exchange that supports efficient cooling for data center infrastructure.

Many landowners possess strategically located hyperscale data center land but lack the technical expertise for data center site selection and data center site preparation to transform it into AI data center infrastructure.

TexasDataCenters.metaTitle: Texas Hyperscale Data Center Infrastructure | CloudTelc
TexasDataCenters.metaDescription: Texas leads the US with 3.4GW capacity. CloudTelc provides expert site selection, high-voltage power & fiber connectivity for hyperscale AI data centers in DFW, Houston, and beyond.
TexasDataCenters.heroTitle: Strategic Infrastructure for Texas Data Centers
TexasDataCenters.heroSubtitle: As of 2026, Texas has solidified its position as the most dynamic data center market in the US, driven by abundant land, competitive energy costs, and a surging demand for AI infrastructure.

TexasDataCenters.leadText: As of 2026, Texas has solidified its position as the most dynamic data center market in the US, driven by abundant land, competitive energy costs, and a surging demand for AI infrastructure.

TexasDataCenters.regionSanAntonioText: San Antonio represents about 10% of the Texas market with growing hyperscale interest. Favorable power and land availability support AI infrastructure site selection and development.
TexasDataCenters.regionAustinText: Austin’s tech ecosystem and talent pool drive demand for hyperscale data center development Texas. CloudTelc supports high-voltage power transmission for data centers and fiber infrastructure planning.
TexasDataCenters.faqTitle: Frequently Asked Questions — Texas Data Centers
TexasDataCenters.faq1Question: What is ERCOT’s role in Texas data center energy demand?
TexasDataCenters.faq1Answer: ERCOT manages the Texas electric grid and balances supply and demand. As data center load grows toward 78 GW by 2030, ERCOT planning and interconnection timelines are critical for hyperscale data center development Texas and high-voltage power transmission for data centers.
TexasDataCenters.faq2Question: How much water do Texas data centers use annually?
TexasDataCenters.faq2Answer: Texas data center cooling and power generation can use on the order of 25 billion gallons of water annually. Site selection and cooling technology choices directly affect water use; CloudTelc’s AI infrastructure site selection considers water availability and sustainability.
TexasDataCenters.faq3Question: What Texas data center tax incentives are available?
TexasDataCenters.faq3Answer: Texas offers various incentives for data center and industrial development, including property tax abatements and economic development agreements. CloudTelc helps investors and developers navigate Texas data center tax incentives as part of site selection and development.
TexasDataCenters.ctaText: Expert site preparation and infrastructure for the future of AI. Partner with CloudTelc for your Texas hyperscale projects.
